heading: Citizen suits
label: 6972 Citizen suits

children.label: A
children.text: against any person (including (a) the United States, and (b) any other governmental instrumentality or agency, to the extent permitted by the eleventh amendment to the Constitution) who is alleged to be in violation of any permit, standard, regulation, condition, requirement, prohibition, or order which has become effective pursuant to this chapter; or

children.label: B
children.text: against any person, including the United States and any other governmental instrumentality or agency, to the extent permitted by the eleventh amendment to the Constitution, and including any past or present generator, past or present transporter, or past or present owner or operator of a treatment, storage, or disposal facility, who has contributed or who is contributing to the past or present handling, storage, treatment, transportation, or disposal of any solid or hazardous waste which may present an imminent and substantial endangerment to health or the environment; or
against the Administrator where there is alleged a failure of the Administrator to perform any act or duty under this chapter which is not discretionary with the Administrator.

children.label: i
children.text: the Administrator;

children.label: ii
children.text: the State in which the alleged violation occurs; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to any alleged violator of such permit, standard, regulation, condition, requirement, prohibition, or order,

children.label: B
children.text: if the Administrator or State has commenced and is diligently prosecuting a civil or criminal action in a court of the United States or a State to require compliance with such permit, standard, regulation, condition, requirement, prohibition, or order.

children.label: i
children.text: the Administrator;

children.label: ii
children.text: the State in which the alleged endangerment may occur;

children.label: iii
children.text: any person alleged to have contributed or to be contributing to the past or present handling, storage, treatment, transportation, or disposal of any solid or hazardous waste referred to in subsection (a)(1)(B),

children.label: i
children.text: has commenced and is diligently prosecuting an action under or under section 106 of the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act of 1980 [],section 6973 of this title42 U.S.C. 96061So in original. The comma probably should be a semicolon.1

children.label: ii
children.text: is actually engaging in a removal action under section 104 of the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act of 1980 [];42 U.S.C. 9604

children.label: iii
children.text: has incurred costs to initiate a Remedial Investigation and Feasibility Study under section 104 of the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act of 1980 [] and is diligently proceeding with a remedial action under that Act [ et seq.]; or42 U.S.C. 960442 U.S.C. 9601

children.label: iv
children.text: has obtained a court order (including a consent decree) or issued an administrative order under section 106 of the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act of 980 [] or pursuant to which a responsible party is diligently conducting a removal action, Remedial Investigation and Feasibility Study (RIFS), or proceeding with a remedial action.242 U.S.C. 9606section 6973 of this titleSo in original. Probably should be “1980”.2

children.label: i
children.text: has commenced and is diligently prosecuting an action under subsection (a)(1)(B);

children.label: ii
children.text: is actually engaging in a removal action under section 104 of the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act of 1980 []; or42 U.S.C. 9604

children.label: iii
children.text: has incurred costs to initiate a Remedial Investigation and Feasibility Study under section 104 of the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act of 1980 [] and is diligently proceeding with a remedial action under that Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 960442 U.S.C. 9601

children.label: D
children.text: No action may be commenced under subsection (a)(1)(B) by any person (other than a State or local government) with respect to the siting of a hazardous waste treatment, storage, or a disposal facility, nor to restrain or enjoin the issuance of a permit for such facility.

children.label: E
children.text: In any action under subsection (a)(1)(B) in a court of the United States, any person may intervene as a matter of right when the applicant claims an interest relating to the subject of the action and he is so situated that the disposition of the action may, as a practical matter, impair or impede his ability to protect that interest, unless the Administrator or the State shows that the applicant’s interest is adequately represented by existing parties.

children.label: F
children.text: Whenever any action is brought under subsection (a)(1)(B) in a court of the United States, the plaintiff shall serve a copy of the complaint on the Attorney General of the United States and with the Administrator.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: c Notice
children.text: No action may be commenced under paragraph (a)(2) of this section prior to sixty days after the plaintiff has given notice to the Administrator that he will commence such action, except that such action may be brought immediately after such notification in the case of an action under this section respecting a violation of subchapter III. Notice under this subsection shall be given in such manner as the Administrator shall prescribe by regulation. Any action respecting a violation under this chapter may be brought under this section only in the judicial district in which such alleged violation occurs.

children.heading: Intervention
children.label: d Intervention
children.text: In any action under this section the Administrator, if not a party, may intervene as a matter of right.

children.heading: Costs
children.label: e Costs
children.text: The court, in issuing any final order in any action brought pursuant to this section or , may award costs of litigation (including reasonable attorney and expert witness fees) to the prevailing or substantially prevailing party, whenever the court determines such an award is appropriate. The court may, if a temporary restraining order or preliminary injunction is sought, require the filing of a bond or equivalent security in accordance with the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.section 6976 of this title

children.heading: Other rights preserved
children.label: f Other rights preserved
children.text: Nothing in this section shall restrict any right which any person (or class of persons) may have under any statute or common law to seek enforcement of any standard or requirement relating to the management of solid waste or hazardous waste, or to seek any other relief (including relief against the Administrator or a State agency).

children.heading: Transporters
children.label: g Transporters
children.text: A transporter shall not be deemed to have contributed or to be contributing to the handling, storage, treatment, or disposal, referred to in subsection (a)(1)(B) taking place after such solid waste or hazardous waste has left the possession or control of such transporter, if the transportation of such waste was under a sole contractual arrangement arising from a published tariff and acceptance for carriage by common carrier by rail and such transporter has exercised due care in the past or present handling, storage, treatment, transportation and disposal of such waste.
